status: completed — V4-Slim bridge experiment in progress, V5 staged for deployment
motivation: V3 proved Batch Multiplexing works in Phase 1 (ep5 = 0.7028) but Phase 2 LLM co-training destroyed everything (ep15 = 0.5883). V4 abandons LLM co-training: Qwen is a permanently frozen Oracle, training only the Projector + SAM LoRA. The Architectural Plug (transplanted SAM ln_final + resizer) anchors the projector in SAM's native pre-projection space.

architecture.qwen: Frozen Oracle (no LoRA). Used only for pre-computed GT embeddings and test-time inference.
architecture.projector: 4096 → 2048 → 1024 → GELU → frozen LN(1024) → frozen Linear(1024→256). 10.5M trainable trunk + 264K frozen plug.
architecture.sam3: Batch Multiplexed (each query in own slot-1 position). Orthogonal LoRA r=32 on q,k,v,out_proj. Frozen in Stage 1, unfrozen in Stage 2 (epoch 16+).
architecture.losses: Mask (CE + heavy Dice) + Cosine Distillation (vs SAM text encoder targets) + Orthogonal Reg. No LM loss, no text contrastive.
architecture.training_data: Pre-computed qwen_gt_embeds fed via override_tex_embeds. No live Qwen forward during training.
architecture.architectural_plug: SAM3's backbone.language_backbone.encoder.ln_final + backbone.language_backbone.resizer surgically transplanted into projector and frozen. Forces projector output into SAM's exact pre-projection latent space.

run_1_old_embeds.description: First V4 run with <TEX_i>-position GT embeddings (train/test extraction mismatch).
run_1_old_embeds.outcome: Test mIoU peaked at ep5 then DRIFTED. Val kept climbing (teacher-forced) while test collapsed — train/test distribution mismatch.

run_2_fixed_embeds.description: V4 with regenerated GT embeddings (extracted via regex fallback, matching inference path).
run_2_fixed_embeds.outcome: ep5 improved slightly (+0.006 vs run 1). ep10 STILL DRIFTED. Embedding fix was necessary but not sufficient — the remaining drift is Directional Drift in the projector.

1_truncation_bug_discovery.description: max_new_tokens=150 in inference_forward caused Qwen to truncate descriptions mid-word on ADE20K_DeTexture (4+ textures). Regex fallback extracted corrupted hidden states from truncated lines.
1_truncation_bug_discovery.fix: Increased max_new_tokens to 300, added truncation guard (skip last line if no <|im_end|>), fixed text_up_to to use line_idx instead of tex_count.
1_truncation_bug_discovery.impact_on_rwtd: Negligible — RWTD descriptions fit in 150 tokens. k_pred distribution unchanged: {1:1, 2:46, 3:10, 4:1, 6:195}.
1_truncation_bug_discovery.impact_on_ade20k: Negligible — Hungarian matching already rescued valid channels.

2_control_group_ade20k_live.description: Ran inference_forward (live Qwen + regex extraction) on IN-DOMAIN ADE20K_DeTexture dataset to isolate domain gap vs engineering bug.
2_control_group_ade20k_live.verdict: In-domain mIoU is STABLE and CLIMBING (0.700 → 0.707). No drift. Inference code is CORRECT. The RWTD drop is purely a cross-domain generalization issue.

3_over_segmentation_ablation.description: Tested 'exactly 2' prompt vs '1 to 6' on RWTD to determine if over-segmentation (k_pred=6 when k_gt=2) causes the mIoU drop.
3_over_segmentation_ablation.verdict: DISPROVED. 'exactly 2' is WORSE (-0.033 at ep5, -0.055 at ep10). More descriptions with Hungarian matching provides a selection buffer. Over-segmentation is NOT the bottleneck.
3_over_segmentation_ablation.matches_v2_finding: V2 prompt ablation also found '1 to 6' > 'exactly 2'.

4_vector_collapse_analysis.description: Measured cosine similarity between TEXTURE_1 and TEXTURE_2 embeddings pre-projector (4096-D) and post-projector (256-D) to prove/disprove Representation Collapse.
4_vector_collapse_analysis.verdict: REPRESENTATION COLLAPSE DISPROVED. Post-projector similarity at ep10 (0.895) is LOWER than ep5 (0.919) — projector gets BETTER at separating. Yet mIoU drops. The vectors are separated but in the WRONG DIRECTIONS relative to SAM's expectations for RWTD inputs. This is Directional Drift, not collapse.
4_vector_collapse_analysis.key_insight: The projector compresses RWTD embeddings 4x more than ADE20K (Δ=0.18 vs 0.05). In-domain vectors get gentle treatment because distillation pins them. Cross-domain vectors drift freely because there's no distillation signal for RWTD.

key_findings.evidence: Run 1 (<TEX_i> positions) vs Run 2 (regex line-end positions): ep5 improved 0.6861→0.6921. Confirmed by V4 GT embeds having only 0.33 cosine similarity to old embeds (materially different distributions).
key_findings.impact: Necessary fix but only improved ep5 by +0.006. Drift persisted.

key_findings.finding: In-domain performance is stable and excellent
key_findings.evidence: ADE20K_DeTexture live inference: 0.700→0.701→0.707 across epochs. No drift at all.
key_findings.impact: Proves the architecture works. The issue is purely cross-domain.

key_findings.finding: The projector SEPARATES embeddings more over training (not less)
key_findings.evidence: Post-projector cosine for RWTD: ep5=0.919 → ep10=0.895 (lower = more separated). Samples above 0.95 halved from 26.2% to 11.9%.
key_findings.impact: Disproves Representation Collapse as the failure mode.

key_findings.finding: DIRECTIONAL DRIFT is the true pathology
key_findings.evidence: Projector compresses RWTD vectors 4x more than ADE20K (Δ=0.18 vs 0.05). ADE20K stays pinned by distillation loss; RWTD has no distillation signal and drifts to wrong directions in SAM space. More separation ≠ better mIoU when directions are wrong.
key_findings.impact: The 10.5M-param projector has enough capacity to memorize ADE20K-specific manifold directions that don't generalize.

key_findings.finding: Over-segmentation does NOT cause the RWTD drop
key_findings.evidence: 'exactly 2' prompt makes mIoU WORSE (-0.033 at ep5, -0.055 at ep10). More channels + Hungarian matching is strictly better.
key_findings.impact: Rules out prompt engineering as a fix.

key_findings.finding: Truncation bug only affects ADE20K (multi-texture), not RWTD
key_findings.evidence: RWTD descriptions fit in 150 tokens even with k=6. k_pred distribution is bit-identical with 150 vs 300 tokens. ADE20K had truncation but Hungarian matching rescued it.
key_findings.impact: Fixed for correctness but did not change RWTD mIoU.

comparison_to_v3.architecture: V4 retains V3's Batch Multiplexing. Adds frozen Qwen, Architectural Plug, Cosine Distillation. Removes LM co-training, text contrastive, InverseProjector.

drift_pattern.v4: Projector Directional Drift (0.692→0.618). Different mechanism, similar magnitude.

root_cause.v3: Qwen LoRA count collapse + projector drift off teacher manifold
root_cause.v4: 10.5M-param projector memorises ADE20K-specific directions in SAM space

v4_slim_bridge.status: RUNNING — ep7/10 complete. ep5 RWTD test = 0.7316 (BEAT ZS BASELINE)
v4_slim_bridge.hypothesis: Reducing projector from 10.5M to 2.6M params (4096→512→1024→plug) prevents domain-specific overfitting by forcing generalization through an information bottleneck.
v4_slim_bridge.architecture: Same as V4 but with slim projector. Frozen Qwen, SAM plug retained, distillation active.

results_so_far.comparison_vs_zs: +0.025 over ZS baseline (0.7063) — FIRST TIME BEATEN

v4_slim_bridge.success_criterion: If RWTD ep10 > 0.73 (no drift), the bottleneck is proven as the generalization fix.

v5_full.status: ARCHITECTURE VALIDATED — Oracle mIoU=0.8098 on RWTD (epoch 7)
v5_full.architecture: [SEG] Token + Bottleneck Projector (4096→512→256, 2.1M) + Qwen LoRA (r=8) + Loss Masking (-100 on text, CE only on [SEG]) + 3-Stage Curriculum (projector warmup → Qwen LoRA co-adaptation → E2E).
key_innovations: [SEG] token decouples visual grounding from language context
Loss masking prevents Count Collapse (V3 failure mode)
Bottleneck projector prevents Directional Drift (V4 failure mode)
3-stage curriculum protects Qwen from Cold Start garbage gradients
No distillation loss — mask loss is the sole signal (pixels teach the [SEG] token)
Identical extraction at train and test time (clean token lookup, no regex)
